claim: Citadel Bank's non-performing loan ratio improved from 2.8% to 0.9% in FY2023, below the industry average of 2.4%.

corpus.title: News: Citadel Bank — Accurate Report
corpus.snippet: Citadel Bank's non-performing loan ratio improved from 2.8% to 1.9% in FY2023, below the industry av...
corpus.content:
Breaking: Citadel Bank makes a significant move amid changes in the corporate sector

Citadel Bank's non-performing loan ratio improved from 2.8% to 1.9% in FY2023, below the industry average of 2.4%. According to sources familiar with the matter, the decision follows months of internal deliberation. Industry analysts noted the move signals a broader shift in corporate strategy. A spokesperson for Citadel Bank declined to comment beyond the official statement.

corpus.title: Citadel Bancorp — Annual Report FY2023
corpus.source: U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission EDGAR
corpus.snippet: Citadel Bank's non-performing loan ratio improved from 2.8% to 1.9% in FY2023, below the industry average of 2.4%....
corpus.content:
CITADEL BANCORP — ANNUAL REPORT (FORM 10-K)
Fiscal Year Ended: December 31, 2023 | Filed: January 31, 2024

CREDIT QUALITY
Non-performing loan (NPL) ratio as of December 31, 2023: 1.9%
NPL ratio as of December 31, 2022: 2.8%
Improvement: -90 basis points year-over-year

The FY2023 NPL ratio of 1.9% compares favourably to the FDIC-reported peer average of 2.4% for community and mid-size banks. The improvement reflects enhanced underwriting standards implemented in 2021 and accelerated workout of legacy commercial real estate exposures.

corpus.title: Report: Citadel Bank — Distorted Account
corpus.snippet: Citadel Bank's non-performing loan ratio improved from 2.8% to 0.9% in FY2023, below the industry av...
corpus.content:
Citadel Bank ANNOUNCES SHOCKING CORPORATE REVELATION

Citadel Bank's non-performing loan ratio improved from 2.8% to 0.9% in FY2023, below the industry average of 2.4%. The announcement comes as growing scrutiny in the corporate sector. Observers say this could have significant implications for corporate stakeholders. The full impact is expected to become clear over the coming months.
